reason: COAREA vs SUBSTITUTION MISLABEL in Step 2: The proof invokes the 'one-dimensional coarea formula' for a smooth diffeomorphism f (monotone, f'>0 everywhere). For a diffeomorphism, the level set f^{-1}(t) is always a singleton, so the sum in (eq coarea-1d) has exactly one term and the formula reduces to the elementary substitution/change-of-variables theorem: integral g(tau) f'(tau) dtau = integral g(f^{-1}(t)) dt. Calling this the 'coarea formula' is technically defensible (the substitution theorem IS a special case) but pedagogically misleading -- it suggests heavier machinery is needed than is actually used. The coarea formula is genuinely needed only for non-injective f (multiple level-set points). This is not an error but an overclaim of necessary tools.
